reviews.review_description: These scenes look amazing, even in my Quest1. This app shows a future where you can walk in a realistic rendering of your future house.

I noticed the Meta Ambassadors on YouTube were hyping up Meta's Hyperscape while ignoring RealityViewer.  RealityViewer looks way better than Hyperscape and has more interesting locations.

One other thing I noticed is that when I search for RealityViewer on Meta's store, it no longer appears for me in the search results.  I am wondering that has anything to do with Meta trying to push their substandard alternative.  I can still see it on Sidequest though, so that's good.

hey developers but what's happening? your app is no longer working! it only loads the first screen, all other environments it gives me "loading error" except in some cases it gives me the option to load one. for example it gives me the possibility of loading the loft in the woods, or an apartment among many. I tried installing and uninstalling the app but it's the same.

------



hey developers! i stay tuned a long time!

update please! 😅

---

heyyyyyy!!!! update please! take my money and update!😄🙏




helllooooo.... we are in the year 2023 and there have been no updates... have you created a free wonder that you don't develop? I'm ready to pay for improvements, new beautiful scenarios, the possibility of deleting the ones I don't like, some birds flying in the sky, a sea with waves and reflections, the possibility of a total roomscale.... and not limited to a cube, or at least the ability to step outside the cube's outlines slightly before having the black screen. 
- 
I would also like to have some nice objects to take with my virtual hands, in handtraking or with controllers, statuettes, furniture objects, things like that, but also larger environments. I have visited your site, and there is never anything new. but why did you stop?

---------


Update2:
will you do other scenarios? I would still like to see incredibly detailed flower meadow scenarios like the first screen, the sea with a slight movement and seagulls in the distance. I often visit your site because I'm hungry for news but can't find updates. I have no problem paying 10-15 dollars for this app, even as it is now I would have paid this amount. certainly some scenarios are not beautiful, the best in my opinion are the home screen with the flowery meadow, the houses in the woods, the house by the sea and the panoramic restaurant with palm trees, I don't like everything else.


Update:
obviously incredible graphics especially for the vegetation, trees and grass with flowers. the square space of each teleport is particularly punishing as soon as you are close to the border and the screen immediately goes black, yet I believe that there would be at least a useful margin of a step or two beyond the border, before real display problems arise , it would be nice to have some margin over the edge of the square. the handtraking is ok for the function limited to teleportation, but the hands are a bit too ballerina compared to the home oculus where they are extremely stable and precise. it would be nice to have a slight movement of some tree among the many stills, some occasional bird that crosses the sky, or that alights on a branch, chirps and flies away. it would be very nice to see a flowing stream, it would be great to see a small animated waterfall with this beautiful vegetation nearby. stones that can be collected, perhaps balanced in front of a stream. small objects in the house that you can grab, thanks


Old:
I think it is mainly modified and improved 3D photogrammetry, while plants and vegetables in general have been made on the PC, because one of the limits of 3D photogrammetry is the poor yield of vegetables, but here they are more than perfect, they are incredible! I'd like to see other things, my favorite scenarios are the flowery meadow at the beginning, the little house by the sea, the panoramic restaurant on the sea, and the two little houses in the woods. all scenarios with a high percentage of plant elements, good job! it would be great to see more where there is so much nature and ancient architecture. it would be nice to be able to vote online for each scenario but above all to have the possibility to delete those that are not of interest. excellent handling of hand traking

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback, these are great ideas! As others have also voiced the desire to have control over which scenes to store on the device, we'll likely add the option to delete/download individual scenes in one of the next updates. Stay tuned and thank you for using our app!

reviews.review_description: Alot of this is very top notch vr enviornments. The first one that loads up features the BEST VR grass I've ever seen. Reality Viewer is quite the apt title.
Graphically the most impressive VR experience.

This is one of the best, if not the best, archviz realtime app I saw on Quest. 3D rendered scenes with very realistic vegetation. I first had some problems with the navigation where only one position worked and the others were black, but when I tried again the second time, all worked very well. I can really recommend this to anyone who would like to visually experience realistic architecture in VR.

developer_response.body:
Hello! Thank you for review! Our email is roomscalestudios@gmail.com

We want to get this fixed as soon as possible however this issue has not happened on our end so far so it would be great if you could write us an email detailing it a bit more if you have the time.

reviews.review_description: This appears to be a demo from a company that does archviz stuff. Outside of a few minor visual artifacts, the models are very clean. I was particularly blown away by the detailed vegetation in several of the scenes; the realism exceeded most PC VR. I wish there were some way to hide the menus in the opening tutorial area (the small glave in the forest). The wild flowers are amazing! It would be the icing on the cake if the ocean in the beach house scene had some movement. I realize, however, that this app is already pushing the limits of standalone VR and any option for realistic water is going to be resource intensive.

The graphics are absolutely insane
The ambient sounds and the hand tracing makes it soo good.

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for using our app as well as your praise - your feedback means a lot to us!

reviews.review_description:
This was great. It made me feel like i was actually there. Great job.
However, i feel as if there is not enough freedom. A free move option would be a great app changer. Just need a boundary around the map. Other than that, everything is so relaxing. Keep up the good work!
someone should make a game with this type of background.. would be amazing... like a co op shooting game like contractor's.

developer_response.body: Thank you for using our app as well as your feedback! Like you, we are thrilled about the possibilities and hope that game-studios will collaborate with us and leverage our technology to bring the next generation of stand-alone VR games to the Quest!

reviews.review_description: Pretty cool app, really immersive and relaxing. I look forward to seeing more. I hope the developer will add a virtual chair that we can sit in and enjoy the  experience, perhaps an option for music and a tv that shows you tube videos would make this app a vr experience you might never want to leave having entered.

I wouldn't be desappinted with such graphics on PC, and the performance is perfect.

Only complaint is that its really easy to accidentally look out of the bounding box and that is distracting. Would be nice to be able to freeroam or at least clip out from the box, even if I need to enable it in some settings menu.

Thanks unity for the work ! 
The details are INSANE, but need more products. I finished all the environment in 20mins.. 
And i add one more negative comment about the moves, we can’t move where we want.

Really great looking scenes. Very immersive. Bending down and looking at the plants in the forest, wow!

I would move into the beach cabin tomorrow.

The scene selection interface is confusing. Text appearing on the left when you select something on the right. No visual cues that the text updates, or that you need to press the open button. I kept double-clicking on the image in the carrousel and nothing happened…

I know you are showcasing your immersive capabilities, placing 3D models in virtual spaces, but if you added a few hotspots/viewpoints on the many sofas or chairs in these models, this app could easily transform into a ‘meditation destination’.

I can see myself peacefully sitting in one of these, music in the background, virtual fire going.

If you really wanted to make this into a ‘destination’ add multiplayer access and a youtube player or web browser on one of the TVs. See Black Hole Pool for a great example.

I would also love a directory of additional 3D models to download (eg some of your clients)

This app is incredible. The level of detail is astounding. Truly immersive. If I could change one thing it would be this: Make the plants on the ground and leaves on the trees move ever so slightly. Nature is never completely still. And if you could make the ocean come alive, that would be the icing on the cake.
